business.headline: Sensex ends 736 points higher, Nifty above 23,800; IndiGo up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex surged 736.38 points, or 0.97%, to settle at 76,264.33, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 231 points, or 0.98%, to close at 23,853.90. The rally was broad-based, with strong buying seen in auto, real estate, industrial and consumption-related stocks.

Why the US-Iran peace deal is good news for IndiaThe reported US-Iran agreement to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz has eased immediate pressure on India's oil-dependent economy. Its broader benefit will depend on whether lower crude prices and calmer trade routes hold.

Strait of Hormuz opens again, but will petrol and diesel prices fall?The Strait of Hormuz has reopened after a US-Iran understanding eased immediate supply concerns. The move may cool crude prices, but any relief in petrol, diesel and LPG is likely to be limited and gradual.

E20 fuel and car insurance: Will your claim be affected?ICICI Lombard has flagged that E20-linked damage claims in older vehicles may not be straightforward. The issue has sharpened concerns over whether fuel-related wear, negligence or policy exclusions could leave owners paying repair costs.

Why crude oil, monsoon and fertiliser prices are worrying the governmentNirmala Sitharaman said India faces risks from crude oil, foreign exchange, fertiliser prices and weaker rainfall. She said the government has kept buffer stocks ready as import and supply pressures persist.

Sensex jumps 1,200 points over US-Iran peace deal: Is worst over for investors?Markets surged after the US and Iran signalled a deal to end hostilities and restore Strait of Hormuz shipping. The rebound tracked falling crude prices, though analysts said investors should remain cautious.

Sensex jumps 1,200 points: Why is stock market rising today?The biggest trigger behind today's rally was a potential peace agreement between the United States and Iran. The two countries indicated that an initial deal had been reached to end the conflict and resume shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

Sensex surges 1,000 points as investors cheer Iran-US peace dealThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,097.05 points, or 1.45%, to 76,625.00 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 333.90 points, or 1.41%, to 23,955.95 as of 9:25 am.

Why your salary is losing the race against home pricesIndia's housing dream is facing its biggest affordability test as rising prices force buyers to choose between bigger debts, longer commutes and delayed dreams.

FDs, SCSS or mutual funds? The best way to invest Rs 50 lakh after retirementRetiring with Rs 50 lakh may sound reassuring, but the real challenge begins after retirement: how do you turn that lump sum into a steady monthly income without running out of money?

US Justice Department okays Paramount's $110 billion Warner Bros takeoverAfter an eight-month review, the DOJ concluded the proposed merger would strengthen competition across streaming, television and film, potentially benefiting consumers, workers and the broader entertainment industry.

SpaceX opens at $150 in strong Nasdaq debut, up 11% from IPO priceSpaceX surged more than 20 per cent in its Nasdaq debut, lifting its valuation above USD 2 trillion as investors piled into the world's biggest IPO. The stock opened at USD 150, signalling strong demand and easing concerns over its market debut.

Groceries, fuel, school fees: Is your monthly budget about to get tighter?School fees, petrol, vegetables, utility bills—the list of monthly expenses never seems to end. As inflation rises, many families are asking the same question: is their budget about to get even tighter?

Inflation rises to 3.93% for May as West Asia conflict pushes up fuel and food pricesFood inflation continued to move higher in May. It stood at 4.78%, up from 4.20% in April, showing that prices of food items are rising faster than they were a month earlier.

Markets rally on easing West Asia tensions, oil falling below $88The S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Salary mismatch to missed interest: 7 ITR mistakes that can trigger tax noticesFiling your ITR may seem straightforward, but even a minor reporting error can attract the attention of the tax department. Here are seven mistakes you should watch out for.

The world's biggest IPO starts trading today. Is Elon Musk's SpaceX overvalued?SpaceX begins trading on Nasdaq after a record $75 billion IPO and a $1.75 trillion valuation. The debut has sharpened questions over valuation, Musk's control, xAI losses and broader market liquidity.

FD rates up to 8.10%: Check these small finance banks offering the highest returnsIn a world of rising uncertainty, guaranteed returns are becoming more valuable. Here's a look at the small finance banks offering FD rates as high as 8.10% this month.

Govt's new petrol, diesel order: Who is affected, and why was it introduced?The Centre has restricted industrial and commercial bulk fuel purchases from retail pumps. The move aims to protect pump supplies for ordinary consumers amid a wide retail-bulk price gap.

Earning Rs 20 lakh? Claim these tax benefits under the new tax regimeThink the new tax regime leaves no room for tax savings? If you earn Rs 20 lakh a year, you may be surprised to learn that several tax-friendly benefits are still available.

entertainment.headline: Top IMDb-rated movies and shows recently added to OTT platforms From Dhurandhar: The Revenge and Made in India: A Titan Story to Sapne vs Everyone and The Boys, several top IMDb-rated movies and shows have recently arrived on OTT platforms. Here's a look at the highest-rated titles worth streaming this week.

Super Subbu: Sundeep Kishan turns sex ed teacher in Netflix's July release Netflix has announced Super Subbu, its first Telugu original series starring Sundeep Kishan, Mithila Palkar and Murali Sharma. The comedy-drama will premiere in July.

Gadar cast then and now: Where are they 25 years later? Gadar: Ek Prem Katha has completed 25 years since its release. Here's what Sunny Deol, Ameesha Patel and the rest of the film's memorable cast are doing today.

We will not rest: Actor Manjari Fadnnis vows justice for society dog Mikey Manjari Fadnnis has vowed to seek justice after the killing of Mikey, a dog from her housing society. She said residents are aiding the police investigation and want other strays in the complex kept safe.

Ariana Grande tears up during Los Angeles show after being overwhelmed by fan love Ariana Grande paused her Los Angeles show to thank fans after an explosive crowd response left her in tears. The moment underlined her emotional return to touring and the lasting bond she says fans have carried through every chapter.

Ajith Kumar's Le Mans race in France gets special cheer from wife Shalini. Watch Wife and former actor Shalini was seen cheering from the stands as Ajith Kumar raced at Le Mans in France. The moment underscored the family's support as his team claimed a top 10 finish.

Lagaan actor Paul Blackthorne recalls Aamir Khan's curry pasta surprise on set As Lagaan completes 25 years, actor Paul Blackthorne recalled the challenges of filming in Bhuj and shared a hilarious food-related memory involving Aamir Khan.

Akhil Akkineni's Lenin postponed again, but this time for Ram Charan's Peddi Akhil Akkineni's Lenin has been postponed again, with the makers moving its release to July 10. The team says that they decided to push the release, seeing Ram Charan's Peddi running successfully in theatres.

After Arijit, Pritam's 'wants to live life differently' post sparks retirement buzz Pritam's reflective birthday message has sparked widespread speculation among fans. As comparisons emerge with Arijit Singh's recent mainstream exit, many are wondering what the composer's cryptic note really means.

China copy: Vir Das claims Zepto delivered a fake Apple Watch, company responds Comedian-actor Vir Das alleged that an Apple smartwatch worth Rs 50,000 bought through Zepto did not appear genuine. He claimed customer support failed to resolve the issue and called the experience a scam.

Dhruva Natchathiram delayed again? Gautham Menon seeks 30-day extension from court Director Gautham Vasudev Menon has sought 30 more days from the Madras High Court to release Dhruva Natchathiram. The move points to another delay for the Vikram-starrer as related appeals await orders.
Wife Sshura Khan's 'limited-edition' reply to troll calling Arbaaz Khan old man Sshura Khan had a witty response after a social media user mocked her husband Arbaaz Khan by calling him an "old man."

Is Mohanlal-Manjummel Boys director film happening? Here's what we know Rumours of a first-time collaboration between Mohanlal and Manjummel Boys director Chidambaram recently grabbed attention. In a recent interview, the filmmaker addressed the rumours.
Kumkum Bhagya actor Sanchita Ugale dies by suicide in Mumbai Television actor Sanchita Ugale of Kumkum Bhagya fame died by suicide in Mumbai on June 14. Investigation into the case is underway.

Composer Anirudh faces flak over collab with Pocso-accused Jani Master for song Composer Anirudh Ravichander's independent single Aravindh has drawn criticism for featuring choreography by Jani Master, who has been accused in a Pocso case. The backlash has intensified because the release comes from his own audio label.

Dharmendra, not Sunny Deol as Tara Singh in Gadar? Director Anil Sharma explains Anil Sharma has revisited the making of Sunny Deol and Ameesha Patel-starrer Gadar: Ek Prem Katha, opening up about the film's casting decisions and the journey behind one of Indian cinema's biggest blockbusters.

Main Vaapas Aaunga Day 3: Imtiaz Ali's film picks up pace over the weekend Main Vaapas Aaunga registered growth on its first Sunday, collecting Rs 2.50 crore on Day 3. The Imtiaz Ali directorial has now earned Rs 5.50 crore nett at the domestic box office.

Rashmika Mandanna wipes Vijay Deverakonda's sweat, fixes moustache in viral clip Rashmika Mandanna wiped Vijay Deverakonda's sweat and fixed his moustache during their Thummanpet visit. The clip went viral as the actors honoured 180 students through The Deverakonda Foundation.

CM Vijay and Sankgeetha's divorce case: Hearing adjourned to August 7 Chief Minister Vijay and Sankgeetha Sornalingam's divorce petition was heard at the Chengalpattu Mahila court on Monday morning. The court adjourned the matter to August 7, keeping the proceedings pending.

general news.headline: 3 missile deaths, a rotting body: Indian seafarers are collateral in Gulf crisisMultiple US attacks on vessels carrying Indian crew in the Strait of Hormuz, including Marivex, Settebello and MT Jalveer, left three Indians dead and several others affected. Amid the chaos at sea, emergency calls reportedly went unanswered, while a separate sailor died aboard MT Celestial amid delayed evacuation and repatriation.

Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman's aide returns from Delhi airport after entry rowBangladesh Prime Minister Tarique Rahman's adviser, Zahed Ur Rahman, was stopped at New Delhi's IGI Airport for over two hours before his India entry was cleared. However, the aide of the BNP chief chose to return to Dhaka via Colombo, according to reports.

What's in the US-Iran peace deal? A look at the 14-point frameworkThe US and Iran have agreed to a draft framework to halt hostilities, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restart nuclear talks. The interim plan offers sanctions relief and a 60-day negotiating window while bigger disputes remain unresolved.

China is cutting 'obsolete' degrees for an AI future. Where does India stand?While Beijing is shutting down thousands of humanities and management courses to feed its AI ambitions, India's universities continue producing millions of graduates in traditional disciplines. The question is no longer academic: are today's degrees preparing students for tomorrow's jobs?

Stanford students walk out during Sundar Pichai's graduation speechStanford University's graduation ceremony saw a group of students walk out during Google CEO Sundar Pichai's keynote address, protesting over the company's reported contracts with government and defence-linked agencies. The event also saw Pichai keep his speech focused on general advice for graduates, avoiding deeper discussion on technology topics.

IND A vs SL A Live: India under pressure as Sri Lanka tighten grip on matchIndia A (IND) vs Sri Lanka A (SL) Live Score, Tri-Series 2026: SL-A (188/5 in 30.3 ovs chasing 266). Sadeera Samarawickrama and Wanuja Sahan have built a strong partnership as the pressure is back on India.

How Kundu couple could turn nondescript NCPI into 5th-biggest party in Lok SabhaThe Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) has never won a single seat, contested just one election in which it secured a total of 822 votes, and has a funding of Rs 1.13 lakh. Yet, the Trinamool Congress (TMC) rebellion might help it become the fifth-largest party in the Lok Sabha. Here's more about the curious case of the NCPI and its Kundu couple.

India's first woman NDA-trained Air Force officer set to be commissionedFlight Cadet Divyanshi Singh is set to become the first woman trained at the NDA to be commissioned into the Indian Air Force's Ground Duty branch.

Nationalist Citizens Party, TMC rebels and the death of ironyWhat happens when a party that campaigned against political turncoats becomes the refuge of defectors? The answer lies in the dramatic rise of the little-known Nationalist Citizens Party of India.

Canadian province ends student route to permanent residency, Indians to be hitManitoba has become the first Canadian province to scrap the Career Employment Pathway (CEP), a popular student-to-permanent residency programme. It has replaced it with a system that prioritises work experience and labour market integration. Indians planning to take the student route to permanent residency will be impacted.
Kumkum Bhagya actor Sanchita Ugale dies by suicide in MumbaiTelevision actor Sanchita Ugale of Kumkum Bhagya fame died by suicide in Mumbai on June 14. Investigation into the case is underway.

NEET whistleblower says re-exam will be leak-proof, but students remain scepticalThe chemistry teacher who exposed the NEET-UG question paper leak in May believes that the government's measures are likely to make the re-examination on June 21 foolproof. Students in Rajasthan's Sikar, the epicentre of the earlier leak, however, are approaching the re-examination with anxiety, uncertainty, and lingering doubts.

Yemen's 'Spider-Man' falls into volcanic crater as daredevil stunt goes wrong, diesYemeni adventure creator Antar Al Absi fell into the Haradhat Damt volcanic crater during a high-risk climb. His death drew tributes online and renewed warnings about basic safety precautions.

Neeraj Chopra set for return after being sidelined by injury for almost a yearJavelin star Neeraj Chopra is set for a return after spending nearly a year on the sidelines. Neeraj will make his return in the Doha Diamond League, set to be hosted on June 19.

Bihar teen hacks NEET aspirants' accounts on portal, diverts money in refund scamThe 19-year-old accused exploited security vulnerabilities and weak passwords to gain unauthorised access to the accounts of NEET-UG candidates and he changed the bank account details linked to students' profiles.

Infosys co-founder backs viral argument on why India hasn't built a ChatGPTInfosys co-founder Kris Gopalakrishnan backed an X post arguing that Indian IT firms are not built to chase frontier AI models like OpenAI. The exchange has revived a wider debate over whether India should prioritise homegrown models or lean on its strengths in services and enterprise technology.

After setting the IPL alight, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi searches for second gearIndia A vs Sri Lanka A, Talent tri-series: 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is yet to go past 50 in the ongoing series in Dambulla. on Monday, he was dismissed for 21 off 14 balls against Sri Lanka A.

Cadaver code: The dead is the teacher here. A nation needs to take lessonsGiving our body for medical studies, knowing it will be dissected multiple times, is the highest form of donation. This is how cadavers are received, preserved, used with respect and disposed of. But India needs to urgently improve the process of final send-off for cadavers, which might help end their scarcity too.

Karnataka MLC polls: Resort politics returns as Congress looks to shield MLAsWith eight candidates in the fray for seven seats, the contest in Karnataka is expected to be closely fought, making cross-voting a key factor in determining the outcome.

3-year-old Tamil Nadu girl sexually assaulted, dies; migrant worker arrestedA missing three-year-old girl in Thiruvallur was found critically injured in a thorny bush and later died in hospital. Police have arrested a 19-year-old Bihar native after residents alleged he lured her away with biscuits.

You wouldn't believe the shocking reason so many people are choosing to solo-maxxAs dating becomes more expensive and emotionally exhausting, more singles are embracing solo-maxxing. But is it a healthy mindset, or simply another form of avoidance?

BJP, JD(S) target Karnataka township project, write to Rahul Gandhi over land grabThe proposed Rs 18,000-crore Bidadi township project has triggered a political showdown in Karnataka, with the BJP and JD(S) writing to Rahul Gandhi seeking his intervention against the acquisition of nearly 7,500 acres of farmland.

Strait of Hormuz opens again, but will petrol and diesel prices fall?The Strait of Hormuz has reopened after a US-Iran understanding eased immediate supply concerns. The move may cool crude prices, but any relief in petrol, diesel and LPG is likely to be limited and gradual.

NEET re-test candidates hit by admit card download glitches, NTA says don't panicThe National Testing Agency (NTA) said nearly 4 lakh candidates have successfully downloaded their NEET UG Re-Exam 2026 admit cards. While some candidates are facing technical issues, the agency has assured that the glitches are being addressed and admit cards will be available soon.

sports.headline: Neeraj Chopra set for return after being sidelined by injury for almost a year Javelin star Neeraj Chopra is set for a return after spending nearly a year on the sidelines. Neeraj will make his return in the Doha Diamond League, set to be hosted on June 19.

F1: Lewis Hamilton gets a heart from Kim Kardashian after winning Barcelona GP Barcelona-Catalunya GP: Lewis Hamilton's first Ferrari win has drawn an immediate public reaction from Kim Kardashian. Her celebratory post, and Hamilton's own comments, have pushed relationship rumours back into the spotlight.

After setting the IPL alight, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi searches for second gear India A vs Sri Lanka A, Talent tri-series: 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is yet to go past 50 in the ongoing series in Dambulla. on Monday, he was dismissed for 21 off 14 balls against Sri Lanka A.

Cyril Gane halts Alex Pereira's bid at history in White House special UFC: Alex Pereira historic bid for three UFC titles in three separate divisions was halted by Frenchman Ciryl Gane, who knocked out his opponent in the second round of the showpiece fight of the night.

VAR official's 'OK hand' gesture during FIFA World Cup broadcast sparks outrage FIFA World Cup 2026: Australian VAR official Shaun Evans was accused online of flashing a reverse OK sign before Germany vs Curacao. The image now tests FIFA's anti-discrimination message as campaigners demand his removal.

Brainfade? Vipraj Nigam penalised twice for running on pitch, India A docked 10 runs Vipraj Nigam was penalised twice for running on the protected area during India A's innings against Sri Lanka A in Dambulla. The repeated breach, after an earlier warning to Anukul Roy, cost India A 10 runs.

FIFA using cricket's tech in World Cup: Snicko saves the day in Sweden vs Tunisia FIFA World Cup 2026: A cricket-style "Snicko" system was used in VAR review to determine whether Alexander Isak had made a faint touch in the build-up to Sweden's fourth goal against Tunisia.

From Chambal to India U19 captain: Yashbardhan Singh Chauhan's journey in blue A 17-year-old from Gwalior has been named India's U-19 captain for the Sri Lanka tour. Behind the appointment lies a father's dream, a mother's prayers, and a principal who saw a leader long before the selectors did.

Goal fest in Monterrey as Sweden put five past Tunisia Yasin Ayari scored twice as Sweden beat Tunisia 5-1 in Monterrey, opening with a stunning early strike he chose not to celebrate. Sweden's control was reinforced by Tunisia's errors and a technology-backed review that allowed Svanberg's record-fast substitute goal to stand.

IND A vs SL A Live: India bounce back as Sri Lanka lose quick wickets India A (IND) vs Sri Lanka A (SL) Live Score, Tri-Series 2026: SL-A (170/5 in 26 ovs chasing 266). Suryansh Shedge has taken the wicket of Ahan Wickramasinghe as India are bouncing back into the contest with quick wickets. Sadeera Samarawickrama remains the key for the home team.

Why didn't Sweden's Yasin Ayari celebrate after scoring against Tunisia? FIFA World Cup 2026: Yasin Ayari fired Sweden ahead against Tunisia with a magnificent early strike and chose not to celebrate. His restraint, shaped by family ties, gave a sharp human edge to a frantic Group F first half.

Somali referee Omar Artan, denied entry to USA, set to be paid in full by FIFAFIFA is set to pay Omar Artan in full despite him not officiating in the FIFA World Cup 2026. The Somalian referee was denied entry to the USA and was sent back from the airport.

Amad Diallo late dagger helps Ivory Coast to 1-0 win over EcuadorFIFA World Cup 2026: Philadelphia was painted in Ecuador's colours and La Tri created enough chances to win. But a late moment of brilliance from Amad Diallo handed Ivory Coast a dramatic 1-0 victory and an early advantage in Group E.

Japan fans win hearts again by cleaning Dallas stadium after Dutch thrillerFIFA World Cup 2026: Japan supporters cleaned the Dallas Stadium stands after their 2-2 World Cup draw with the Netherlands. The gesture went viral again and highlighted a long-running culture of the Blue Samurai fans.

Lamine Yamal likely off bench for Spain's World Cup opener against Cape VerdeFIFA World Cup 2026: Spain have received a fitness boost ahead of their World Cup opener, but fans may have to wait a little longer to see Lamine Yamal from the start. Luis de la Fuente confirmed the teenager is fit but not ready for a full match.

Iran land in US for FIFA World Cup opener after landmark peace dealIran's World Cup campaign has unfolded amid extraordinary political circumstances. Team Melli arrived in Los Angeles ahead of their opener against New Zealand just hours before a US-Iran peace deal was announced.

What's in that notebook? Fans think Japan coach has a Death NoteJapan coach Hajime Moriyasu's tactical changes against the Netherlands reignited the famous Death Note meme, leaving many fans wondering about the story behind the joke.

SWE 5-1 TUN, World Cup Highlights: Sweden beat Tunisia with some comfort at Stadio MonterreySweden (SWE) vs Tunisia (TUN) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Sweden cruised to a comfortable win over Tunisia as they scored five at the Stadio Monterrey, thanks to Isak, Gyokeres, Ayari and Svanberg. Tunisia got one back through Rekik before half-time but Sweden looked comfortable for the victory.

Japan earn deserved draw as Dutch pay for defensive approachFIFA World Cup 2026: Japan came from behind twice to earn a deserved 2-2 draw against the Netherlands, punishing a Dutch side that retreated after taking control of their Group F clash.

A nod to Hanuman ji, Deepti Sharma scripts another ICC event masterclassDeepti Sharma dragged India past Pakistan with a decisive spell in the Women's T20 World Cup. The performance underlined her habit of peaking on the ICC stage despite lean form coming in.

Curacao's World Cup dream lives on despite crushing Germany defeatFIFA World Cup 2026: Curacao coach Dick Advocaat urged his players to stay positive after a 7-1 defeat to Germany, saying the nation's historic World Cup debut remains a moment of pride.

Germany spoils Curacao's World Cup debut with 7-1 hammeringGermany overcame an early scare to demolish Curacao 7-1 in Houston in their opening contest on Sunday, June 14. A brace from Kai Havertz ensured that Germany started off on the right note in the tournament.

Who is Livano Comenencia? Curacao hero scores historic World Cup goal vs GermanyFIFA World Cup 2026: Livano Comenencia scored Curacao's first World Cup goal as the debutants drew level against Germany in Houston. The strike triggered wild celebrations and marked a landmark moment for the tournament's smallest qualified nation.

Richa Ghosh as opener? Harmanpreet in awe of India batter after Pakistan cameoWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs PAK: Richa Ghosh starred with a blistering 34 off 17 balls to power India to a commanding 64-run win over Pakistan in their opening game on Sunday.

India strangle Pakistan with spin to begin T20 World Cup with statement winWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs PAK: Harmanpreet Kaur's India made a stupendous start to their campaign after hammering Pakistan by 64 runs on Sunday. Smriti Mandhana laid the platform with a fifty, after which Deepti Sharma took five wickets.

NED 2-2 JAP, FIFA World Cup Highlights: Japan steal one point against the DutchNetherlands vs Japan, FIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: (NED 2-2 JAP) Japan earned a huge point in Group F after twice coming from behind to hold the Netherlands to a thrilling 2-2 draw in Arlington. Goals from Ayase Ueda and late substitute Koki Ogawa cancelled out strikes from Donyell Malen and Crysencio Summerville in a pulsating second half.

Manpreet Singh equals Dilip Tirkey's India record as caps controversy endsManpreet Singh matched Dilip Tirkey's Indian record of 412 international caps against the Netherlands in Rotterdam. The appearance ends months of speculation over whether he would be stopped from surpassing the mark.

F1 2026: Lewis Hamilton ends Ferrari wait, storms to Barcelona GP winLewis Hamilton took his first Ferrari victory at the Barcelona Grand Prix after a late virtual safety car swung strategy decisively his way. Kimi Antonelli's late retirement handed George Russell second and produced a rare all-British podium.

How Ronaldo, Portugal plan to pay tribute to Diogo Jota at World Cup 2026Portugal will wear commemorative wristbands for Diogo Jota during their World Cup campaign. Ronaldo and the rest of the team will be wearing the wristbands during matches as they chase their first World Cup.

Bangladesh survive Netherlands scare to begin Women's T20 World Cup with record chaseBangladesh overcame a spirited challenge from the Netherlands to begin their Women's T20 World Cup campaign with a six-wicket win, completing their highest-ever successful chase in the tournament.

Gun Gurnoor: 6ft 5in pacer makes a mighty impression in DharamsalaIndia fast bowler Gurnoor impressed on his debut for India on Saturday, June 13. Playing against Afghanistan, Gurnoor bowled at high pace, justifying his fast tracking to the Indian cricket team.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
